steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that longitudinal studies like NEPS require extensive funding to collect data "over timescales and at intervals." The blank must complete the contrast introduced by "But" — explaining why a transportation study seeking only average daily usage is much less dependent on high funding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'such studies are not trying to identify trends over time': Directly explains the contrast. A one-time count of daily transit users doesn't need repeated measurements across years, so it avoids the expensive timescale-and-interval requirement that drives NEPS's funding needs. ✓
  • Choice B — 'such studies are likely to be a lower priority for funding than NEPS': Discusses funding priority between the two studies, not why the transportation study is less dependent on high funding. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the transportation study is unlikely to be able to recruit 60,000 participants': Introduces participant count, which the passage doesn't link to funding dependence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'expanding the scope of such studies is unlikely to be feasible': Scope expansion is never connected to funding levels in the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
